response.justification: Mohammed is a technically strong individual contributor with genuine developer tooling experience (CI/CD infrastructure, test frameworks, MCP tooling) and broad language depth accumulated over roughly 20 years — but this role is fundamentally a people management position. There is no evidence anywhere in the resume of managing direct reports, conducting performance reviews, running 1:1s, or carrying organizational accountability for a team. The 'Staff' titles at Altom and re:cinq appear to reflect IC seniority, not managerial scope. Without meeting the 2-year people management minimum — which is arguably the most load-bearing requirement for a Level II Engineering Manager — the profile is a poor fit regardless of his technical credentials.
strengths: Hands-on developer tooling experience: built CI servers, GitLab infrastructure, a D-language test runner (Trial), assertion library (Fluent-Asserts), and MCP code generators
Exceptionally broad technical range across web, mobile, embedded, and data-intensive systems — relevant for a multi-discipline productivity mandate
Strong knowledge-sharing track record via community workshops, conference talks, and open-source projects, suggesting coaching instincts that could translate to mentorship
concerns: Zero demonstrated people management: no direct reports, no hiring, no performance management, no org structure ownership anywhere in the resume
No evidence of setting or executing a multi-quarter technical roadmap for a team, which is a core responsibility for this role
Mobile platform depth is shallow and dated (Apple TV/iOS app at WatchUp, 2015–2016) — insufficient for a manager overseeing a major mobile platform ecosystem
